author: gaxanh
content:
According to the current spec, clearing a slot will be refunded and creating a new slot will be charged state creation fee, while updating a slot still applies a storage write fee, even though the net state of both is zero. Two approaches:
1. Keep it as is, this is simple for implementation
2. Charge based on the net state change, counting the total number of cleared slots and the total number of created slots and only applying the storage write fee to both slots (referred to as cross-updating). If the remainder are created slots, a state creation fee will be charged; otherwise, a refund will be applied. More complex to implement, but this is a common use case (e.g., moving balances to another account)
